content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

Found two correctness gaps in the new exhaustion handling, both in `process_backend`: exit-0 Claude error envelopes that report exhaustion via `errors[]`/`subtype` still fall into schema-validation retries, and reset-time rate-limit detection is limited to the literal `try again at` wording.

## Amendments

1. In `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:157-171`, stop looking only at `envelope.result` when an exit-0 Claude envelope has `is_error: true`. `extract_stdout_error` in `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1632-1650` already knows how to pull error text from `result`, `errors[]`, and `subtype`, but the new exhaustion path bypasses that helper. As written, an exit-0 envelope like `{"is_error":true,"errors":["quota exceeded"]}` or a subtype-only exhaustion error will fall through into normal payload parsing, become a schema-validation failure, and still retry instead of degrading gracefully.
   Rationale: This leaves a real backend-exhaustion shape unhandled, so the run can still burn retries and fail the whole stage even though the new failure class exists.
2. Broaden the persistent-rate-limit detection in `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1842-1848`. The current implementation only upgrades to `BackendExhausted` when the text contains both `rate limit` and the exact phrase `try again at`. Reset-time variants such as `retry after 2h`, `until 2026-04-06T15:03Z`, or other timestamp/duration wordings will still be classified as `TransportFailure` and retried.
   Rationale: The bead explicitly calls for handling persistent rate limits that include a reset time. Right now the matcher only covers one phrasing, so a large class of genuine reset-time exhaustion responses is still missed.
